questions.q: Which of the following alkenes shows geometrical isomerism?
questions.hint: Geometrical isomerism requires each doubly bonded carbon to have two different groups. But-2-ene: CH₃-CH=CH-CH₃ → cis & trans.

questions.q: The IUPAC name of the trans isomer of pent-2-ene is:
questions.hint: In trans (E isomer), higher priority groups on opposite sides. (E)-pent-2-ene.

questions.q: Which of the following has the highest electron affinity?
questions.hint: Chlorine has highest electron affinity (though F has smaller size, Cl wins due to less electron repulsion).

questions.q: Ionization energy generally increases across a period because:
questions.hint: Increasing nuclear charge pulls electrons tighter → more energy to remove an electron.

questions.q: Acetic acid (CH₃COOH) is more acidic than ethanol because:
questions.hint: -I effect of O and C=O stabilizes CH₃COO⁻ by delocalizing negative charge.

questions.q: Which of the following shows resonance effect?
questions.hint: Conjugated diene (1,3-butadiene) has resonance due to alternating double bonds.

questions.q: Octane number 75 means the fuel mixture behaves like:
questions.hint: Octane number = % iso-octane in mixture with n-heptane. 75 = 75% iso-octane, 25% n-heptane.

questions.q: Which is NOT a characteristic of homologous series?
questions.hint: Homologous series members differ by CH₂, so molecular mass increases by 14u each step.

questions.q: Bleaching action of SO₂ is due to:
questions.hint: SO₂ bleaches by reduction (removes oxygen from colored compounds). Cl₂ bleaches by oxidation.

questions.q: In the contact process for H₂SO₄, the catalyst used is:
questions.hint: Vanadium pentoxide (V₂O₅) at ~450°C. Modern process replaces Pt.

questions.q: Nascent hydrogen is a stronger reducing agent than molecular H₂ because:
opts: It is formed in situ with high energy
questions.hint: Nascent hydrogen is atomic hydrogen released during a reaction – highly reactive due to unpaired electron.

questions.q: Which reagent is used to distinguish between alkane and alkene?
questions.hint: Alkene decolorizes bromine water (addition reaction); alkane does not react.

questions.q: Acetylene shows acidic character because:
opts: It has terminal H attached to sp carbon
questions.hint: sp hybridized carbon is more electronegative, so C-H bond is polar – H can be removed by strong bases.

questions.q: Which reaction is used to increase the number of carbon atoms in an alkane?
questions.hint: Wurtz: 2R-X + 2Na → R-R + 2NaX (doubles carbon atoms).

questions.q: Which of the following is isoelectronic with Na⁺?
questions.hint: Na⁺ (10 e⁻), Mg²⁺ (10 e⁻), O²⁻ (10 e⁻), F⁻ (10 e⁻) – all isoelectronic. Size order: O²⁻ > F⁻ > Na⁺ > Mg²⁺.

questions.q: Which of the following is a macro-nutrient for plants?
questions.hint: Macro: N, P, K, Ca, Mg, S. Micro: Fe, Cu, Zn, Mn, B, Mo, Cl.

questions.q: Which reaction is used to prepare alkanes from alkyl halides by doubling the number of carbon atoms?
questions.hint: Wurtz reaction: 2R-X + 2Na → R-R + 2NaX. Doubles carbon chain.

questions.q: Bayer's reagent (dilute KMnO₄) is used to test for:
questions.hint: Bayer's reagent (alkaline KMnO₄) decolorizes in presence of double/triple bonds.

questions.q: According to Markovnikov's rule, when HBr adds to propene, the major product is:
questions.hint: H adds to carbon with more H atoms. CH₃-CH=CH₂ + HBr → CH₃-CHBr-CH₃ (2-bromopropane).

questions.q: Which of the following compounds is aromatic according to Huckel's rule?
questions.hint: Huckel: (4n+2) π electrons, planar, cyclic. Benzene: 6 π e⁻ (n=1).

questions.q: The ring test for nitrate ion involves formation of:
questions.hint: Nitrate + FeSO₄ + conc. H₂SO₄ → brown ring of nitroso iron(II) sulfate.

questions.q: Which law of stoichiometry is directly used to deduce Avogadro's hypothesis?
questions.hint: Avogadro used Gay-Lussac's volume ratios to propose equal volumes → equal molecules.

questions.q: The ideal gas equation PV = nRT, the value of R (universal gas constant) is:
questions.hint: R = 0.0821 L·atm·mol⁻¹·K⁻¹ = 8.314 J·mol⁻¹·K⁻¹ = 1.987 cal·mol⁻¹·K⁻¹.

questions.q: According to VSEPR theory, the shape of NH₃ molecule is:
questions.hint: NH₃: 3 bond pairs + 1 lone pair → trigonal pyramidal (like a tetrahedron missing one vertex).

questions.q: Le-Chatelier's principle predicts that increasing pressure on the reaction N₂(g) + 3H₂(g) ⇌ 2NH₃(g) will:
questions.hint: Higher pressure favors fewer gas moles. Left: 4 moles, right: 2 moles → shifts right.

questions.q: Which of the following solids shows anisotropy?
questions.hint: Anisotropy (different properties in different directions) is shown by crystalline solids, not amorphous.

questions.q: The number of sodium hydroxide molecules present in 40 gm of it is:
questions.hint: Molar mass NaOH = 40g = 1 mole = 6.023×10²³ molecules

questions.q: When CO gas is heated with Fehling's solution, red ppt. of ______ is formed:
questions.hint: Fehling's test reduces Cu²⁺ to Cu₂O (red precipitate)

questions.q: The microelement present in diet is:
questions.hint: Microelements = trace elements; Cu, Zn, I, etc. Mg and Na are macro.

questions.q: The inflammable compound among the following is:
questions.hint: PH₃ (phosphine) is highly inflammable, burns with luminous flame.

questions.q: In Down's process for extraction of Sodium:
opts: C is cathode & Fe is anode
C is anode & Pt is cathode
Pt is anode & Fe is cathode
C is anode & Fe is cathode
questions.hint: Down's cell: Graphite anode, Iron cathode; NaCl fused at 600°C.

questions.q: Setting of plaster is accompanied with:
questions.hint: Plaster of Paris (CaSO₄·½H₂O) sets by hydration → CaSO₄·2H₂O (Gypsum).

questions.q: Which of the following diffuses most rapidly?
questions.hint: Graham's Law: Rate ∝ 1/√M. NH₃=17 (lightest), diffuses fastest.

questions.q: Which allotrope of carbon can be used to make superconductors?
questions.hint: Fullerene (C₆₀) doped with alkali metals becomes superconducting.

questions.q: The IUPAC name of (CH₃)₂C=C(CH₃)₂ is:
questions.hint: 4 carbons in chain, double bond at C2, methyl groups at C2,C3.

questions.q: When Cl₂ reacts with hot & conc. NaOH, the product is:
questions.hint: Hot conc: 3Cl₂+6NaOH→5NaCl+NaClO₃+3H₂O. Cold dilute gives NaCl+NaClO.

questions.q: Which of the following oxides is amphoteric?
questions.hint: ZnO & Al₂O₃ are amphoteric (react with both acids & bases).

questions.q: The hybridization in graphite is:
questions.hint: Graphite: each C bonded to 3 others in hexagonal layers → sp².

questions.q: Shape of orbital is given by which quantum number?
questions.hint: Azimuthal (l) = shape; Principal (n) = size/energy; Magnetic (m) = orientation.

questions.q: Which property of alkaline earth metals increases with atomic number?
questions.hint: Down group 2: IE↓, electronegativity↓, sulphate solubility↓, hydroxide solubility↑.

questions.q: The number of sigma and pi bonds in benzene is:
questions.hint: Benzene: 6 C-C σ + 6 C-H σ = 12σ; 3 delocalized π bonds.

questions.q: Bauxite ore is concentrated by:
questions.hint: Bauxite (Al₂O₃·2H₂O) purified by Bayer's process (leaching with NaOH).

questions.q: The incomplete period in periodic table is:
questions.hint: 7th period is incomplete (ongoing discoveries of superheavy elements).

questions.q: The oxidation number of Fe in K₃[Fe(CN)₆] is:
questions.hint: K=+1 ×3=+3; CN=-1 ×6=-6; Fe must be +3 to balance: +3+3-6=0.

questions.q: The compound that contains both ionic and covalent bonds is:
questions.hint: KCN: K⁺[C≡N]⁻ — ionic K⁺...CN⁻ and covalent C≡N triple bond.

questions.q: Which compound is assigned octane number 100?
questions.hint: iso-octane (2,2,4-trimethylpentane) = 100; n-heptane = 0.

questions.q: Alkali metals can be extracted from their salts by:
opts: Electrolysis of aqueous solution of their halides
questions.hint: Alkali metals are highly reactive; extracted by electrolysis of molten (fused) halides.

questions.q: IUPAC name of CH₂=CH-CH₂-CH₂-OH is:
questions.hint: 4-carbon chain, double bond at C3, -OH at C1 → but-3-en-1-ol. Lowest locant for -OH.

questions.q: A radioactive isotope of hydrogen is:
questions.hint: Tritium (³H) is radioactive (β-emitter, half-life ~12.3 years).

questions.q: Tetrabromoethane on heating with zinc gives:
questions.hint: Dehalogenation: C₂H₂Br₄ + 2Zn → C₂H₂ (ethyne) + 2ZnBr₂.

questions.q: White phosphorus reacts with caustic alkali to give:
questions.hint: P₄ + 3NaOH + 3H₂O → PH₃ (phosphine) + 3NaH₂PO₂.

questions.q: How many pi-electrons are present in benzene?
questions.hint: 6 π-electrons (each double bond contributes 2; 3×2=6 delocalized).

questions.q: The formula of bleaching powder is:
questions.hint: Bleaching powder = CaOCl₂ (calcium oxychloride), made from Cl₂+Ca(OH)₂.

questions.q: Paste of lime in water is called:
questions.hint: Milk of lime = suspension of Ca(OH)₂ in water. Lime water = clear solution.

questions.q: Weight of one molecule of CaCO₃ is:
questions.hint: Molar mass=100g/mol; 1 molecule=100/6.023×10²³=1.66×10⁻²²g.

questions.q: Structure of NaCl crystal is:
questions.hint: NaCl = FCC lattice (both Na⁺ & Cl⁻ form interpenetrating FCC).

flashcards.back:
Kp = Kc(RT)^Δn
Δn = moles(gaseous products) - moles(gaseous reactants)
If Δn=0, Kp=Kc.

m ∝ Q
m = Z × I × t
Z = E/96500 (E=equivalent mass)
1 Faraday = 96500 C.

Aromatic if:
1. Planar cyclic
2. (4n+2) π electrons
Benzene: n=1, 6π e⁻ ✅
Cyclobutadiene: 4π e⁻ ❌

N₂+3H₂⇌2NH₃+heat
Conditions: 200-300 atm, 450-500°C, Fe catalyst
Le Chatelier: High P, moderate T.

React with BOTH acids & bases:
ZnO, Al₂O₃, BeO, PbO
ZnO+2HCl→ZnCl₂+H₂O
ZnO+2NaOH→Na₂ZnO₂+H₂O

-OH > -CHO > -COOH > double bond > triple bond
CH₂=CH-CH₂-CH₂-OH = but-3-en-1-ol
Lowest number to -OH.

σ: head-on overlap, stronger, free rotation
π: sideways overlap, weaker, no rotation
Benzene: 12σ + 3π bonds

H adds to C with MORE H atoms.
CH₃-CH=CH₂ + HBr → CH₃-CHBr-CH₃ (major)
Peroxide effect reverses it (Anti-Markovnikov).

V ∝ T (at constant P)
V₁/T₁ = V₂/T₂
Absolute zero = -273.15°C = 0K
At 0K, volume theoretically becomes zero.

Reactant that gets consumed FIRST.
Find moles of each; divide by coefficient.
Smallest value = limiting.
Excess = initial - consumed.

Fe in K₃[Fe(CN)₆] = +3
S in H₂S₂O₈ = +6
O = -2 (except peroxides=-1)
H = +1 (except hydrides=-1)
Sum = charge of species.

cheatsheet.title: Must-Know Formulas
items: NTP Volume: 1 mole gas = 22.4 L at NTP

cheatsheet.title: Common Confusions
items: Microelements: Cu, Zn, I; Macro: Mg, Na, Cl

groups.title: GROUP 'A' — MCQs [11×1=11]
questions: Alkali metal extraction method? (Electrolysis of fused halides)
Oxidation number of Fe in K₃[Fe(CN)₆]? (+3)
Shape of orbital — which quantum number? (Azimuthal)

groups.title: GROUP 'B' — Short Questions [8×5=40]
groups.description: Key topics: Charles' Law + CO₂ volume calc | Redox balancing (Zn+HNO₃) | Kp-Kc derivation | Faraday's 1st Law | Graham's diffusion (CₙH₂ₙ₊₂ vs SO₂) | Ostwald's process principle | Hückel's rule | Lewis structures + geometry | Nascent hydrogen reactions | Homologous series + octane number.

groups.title: GROUP 'C' — Long Questions [3×8=24]
questions: Q11: Contact Process (H₂SO₄) — 4 steps, flow sheet, dehydrating nature, SO₄²⁻ test.
Q12: Stoichiometry — 20g marble (90% CaCO₃) + 50g HCl (36.5%) → limiting reactant, excess moles, CO₂ volume, NaOH mass to absorb CO₂.
Q13: Ozonolysis: alkene X → Y(C₂H₄O) + Z(CH₂O). Identify, IUPAC, HBr addition, prep from 1-bromopropane, unsaturation test, convert to propane.
OR: CO₂ polarity, Diamond vs Graphite, Passivity of Iron, Micro/Macronutrients.

examTips.title: The "Last 24 Hours" Strategy
items: Sleep is NON-NEGOTIABLE: 6-7 hours minimum. Your brain consolidates memory during sleep. Pulling an all-nighter drops performance by 30-40%.
Active Recall > Reading: Close the book and say it out loud. Quiz yourself. Reading passively tricks your brain into thinking you know it.
Teach a wall: Explain concepts to an imaginary person. If you can't explain it simply, you don't know it yet.

examTips.title: Marks Hunting Strategy (Target: 55+)
items: MCQs (11 marks): Easy pickings. Do all 30 in this quiz 2-3 times. You'll memorize patterns. Aim for 9-10/11.
Short Questions (40 marks): 5 "sure-shot" topics = 25 marks minimum. Focus: Charles' Law, Redox balancing, Kp-Kc, Faraday's Law, Graham's diffusion.
Long Questions (24 marks): Flow sheet diagrams are FREE marks. Draw them neat. Contact/Solvay/Haber process = guaranteed question.
Partial marks: Even if you can't solve fully, write formulas, draw the flow chart, write the principle. NEB gives step marks.

examTips.title: Exam Hall Playbook
items: First 5 mins: Scan entire paper. Mark "easy" questions. Start with what you know best.
MCQs first: 15 minutes max. If stuck, eliminate 2 options, guess from remaining 2. Move on.
Short answers: 80 minutes. Write point-wise. Underline keywords. Diagrams where possible.
Long answers: 60 minutes. Flow sheets take 3-4 minutes to draw — DO THEM. They're worth 2-3 marks alone.
Last 10 mins: Review. Check units (L, atm, K). Fill any blanks — NEVER leave a question unanswered.

examTips.title: How 55 Marks Break Down
items: 5 Short Qs: 3-4 done well (5 each) + 2 partial (3 each) = 21-26 marks
3 Long Qs: 1 perfectly (7-8) + 2 partial (5-6 each) = 17-20 marks
Total: 46-55 marks — RIGHT where you need to be!
